newsSince: No fresh fundamental news since June 9. The record Q1 2026 results and accelerated/raised 2026 targets (up to EUR1.05B sales, 44-46% EBITDA margin, ~38% of revenue AI-related) were disclosed May 14-15 and already captured by the June 8 verdict. The +18% move since June 9 is momentum/capacity-narrative reiteration, not a new catalyst (UBS 'buy' initiation dates to Sept 2025).

sources.label: Technoprobe surges 36% on Q1, raised targets (Investing.com)
Technoprobe shares surge on raised guidance (Bloomberg)

verdicts.rankRationale: Through the CPO/advanced-packaging-test lens the probe-card scarcity thesis is intact, but no new fundamental evidence landed in the June 9-23 window. The +18% surge restates the May guidance raise already priced June 8; ~30% probe-card share and HBM/chiplet test demand keep it a hold, not a fresh upgrade.
verdicts.bull: ~30% global probe-card share and ~38-50% AI revenue mix make it a genuine choke-point for testing AI/photonic devices; targets pulled forward to 2026.
verdicts.neutral: Strong setup but +180% YTD and a $28B cap have largely captured the upgraded targets; move since June 9 is sentiment.
verdicts.bear: Customer concentration, cyclicality and a steep valuation; any AI capex pause hits a high-multiple test name hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: HBM/chiplet & photonic test demand; capacity doubling 2026-27
verdicts.primaryRisk: Customer concentration / cyclicality at a rich multiple
